title: [P1] Add end-to-end OpenTelemetry instrumentation and trace/log deep links
description:
## Summary

MoonMind has a partial OpenTelemetry foundation, not end-to-end operational tracing.

Merged work already gives Temporal workers a service resource, the Temporal SDK tracing interceptor, Prometheus worker telemetry, and log filters that inject trace/span and Temporal execution context (#1036). Structured worker logs and durable incident manifests also carry useful correlation fields, including durable trace references (#2630). However, those trace references are not proof that one exported trace exists across API, Temporal, activities, providers, artifacts, managed runtimes, and the Omnigent bridge.

The current code path does not yet provide a shared telemetry bootstrap, OTLP exporters, FastAPI instrumentation, consistent API-to-Temporal propagation, domain spans across all side-effect boundaries, or production trace/log deep links.

## Goal

An operator should be able to start at a workflow, run, step execution, incident, provider call, Omnigent bridge session, or log row and navigate to the same correlated trace and bounded logs. Telemetry failures must never change workflow correctness, Temporal determinism, or artifact durability.

The implementation must follow `docs/Observability/OpenTelemetrySystem.md`: Temporal Visibility remains the query plane, artifacts remain the large-evidence plane, and OpenTelemetry is the operational telemetry plane.

## Current baseline

Already shipped:

- Optional Temporal worker tracing behind `MOONMIND_ENABLE_OPENTELEMETRY`.
- `service.name` resource assignment and `temporalio.contrib.opentelemetry.TracingInterceptor`.
- Temporal runtime Prometheus metrics for worker/task-queue health.
- Worker log enrichment with trace ID, span ID, workflow ID, run ID, activity ID, and managed-session context.
- Durable correlation/trace refs in incident reconstruction and step evidence where available (#2630).
- Structured logging and bounded artifact references for large evidence.

Remaining gaps:

- No shared telemetry initialization used by API, workers, auxiliary services, and local tooling.
- No configured OTLP span exporter/processor in the application path; the current SDK provider can create spans without exporting them.
- No FastAPI/HTTP client/database instrumentation baseline.
- Incomplete W3C trace-context propagation from API requests into every Temporal start/signal/update/cancel path and through Continue-As-New/child workflows.
- No consistent activity/provider/tool/sandbox/artifact/Omnigent bridge span contract.
- No operator-configurable trace and log backend URL templates in execution read models.
- No end-to-end integration test proving that one request yields one navigable correlated trace.
- No production sampling, redaction, exporter-health, or collector-outage rollout evidence.

## Architecture decisions

- Workflow code must not perform exporter I/O or depend on non-deterministic telemetry state.
- Temporal client/worker interceptors own orchestration propagation; rich spans belong in activities and side-effecting services.
- Use OTLP as the default application export boundary. The OpenTelemetry Collector owns batching, retries, enrichment, and backend routing.
- Large prompts, model outputs, diffs, stdout/stderr, presigned URLs, and secrets never belong in span attributes. Store them as authorized artifacts and attach bounded refs.
- Stable logical execution identity (`moonmind.correlation_id`) is distinct from Temporal `workflow_id`, Temporal `run_id`, and physical trace IDs.
- Continue-As-New creates a new run-scoped trace segment linked to the prior segment while preserving the stable correlation ID.
- The Omnigent bridge is the target managed-runtime boundary. Instrument bridge/session/event/resource/control calls and propagate context to stock Omnigent when the protocol supports it; do not fork the host solely for telemetry.

## Implementation plan

### 1. Finalize settings and the shared telemetry bootstrap

Create one reusable telemetry package initialized by every MoonMind process. Define typed settings for:

- enablement and environment;
- OTLP endpoint/protocol/headers/TLS;
- service name/version/instance and worker fleet;
- trace sampling policy;
- metric export mode;
- log correlation/export mode;
- payload-capture gates, defaulting off;
- shutdown/flush timeouts;
- backend deep-link templates.

Add required exporter/instrumentation dependencies explicitly. Initialization must be idempotent, safe in tests, and fail open with bounded health diagnostics when the collector is unavailable.

### 2. Add a deployable collector/backend baseline

Check in a supported local/production-shaped example for:

- OpenTelemetry Collector receiving OTLP over gRPC/HTTP;
- Tempo or another trace backend;
- Prometheus-compatible metrics;
- Loki-compatible logs where enabled;
- Grafana data sources and link templates.

The collector, not application code, should own batching, retries, memory limits, attribute processing, and vendor export. Document external-backend overrides without coupling MoonMind code to one vendor.

### 3. Instrument API ingress and outbound clients

Instrument FastAPI request handling with route-template names and safe bounded attributes. Cover:

- execution create/read/action routes;
- Workflow Chat and intervention routes;
- artifact APIs;
- Omnigent bridge/session/event/resource routes;
- provider-profile and OAuth control routes.

Add child spans for outbound HTTP/database calls only where they add diagnostic value and do not leak URLs/query text. Suppress health/readiness noise and apply a route allowlist for attributes.

### 4. Complete API → Temporal propagation

Use the Temporal client interceptor on every client construction path. Propagate W3C trace context plus MoonMind correlation metadata for:

- workflow starts;
- signals;
- updates;
- queries where traced;
- cancels/terminations;
- child workflow starts;
- Continue-As-New links.

Audit all client factories and background launch paths so no route silently creates an uninstrumented client. Keep workflow spans shallow and deterministic.

### 5. Add domain spans at side-effect boundaries

Provide small helpers and semantic conventions for:

- activity invocation and retry attempts;
- provider/model requests and streaming;
- provider-profile lease acquisition/release/cooldown;
- direct managed-runtime launch/session/turn/control compatibility;
- Omnigent bridge session creation, event posting/streaming, elicitation resolution, resource harvest, host lease/readiness, and cleanup;
- artifacts create/read/write/link/preview;
- sandbox/git/tool/MCP execution;
- checkpoint capture/restore and recovery preflight;
- outbound side-effect policy decisions.

Each span must carry the applicable workflow/run/step-execution/attempt/agent-run/bridge-session identities, outcome class, retry count, duration, and artifact refs. Do not add unbounded identifiers to metric labels.

### 6. Standardize trace-correlated logs

Extend the existing structured logging path so API and every worker/service emit the same required fields. Bind trace/span context automatically, preserve explicit MoonMind execution context, and redact secret-like keys and values before serialization.

Full runtime logs remain artifact/Live Logs evidence; application logs contain bounded summaries and refs. Add contract tests that forbidden payload fields never enter logs or spans even when content capture is enabled incorrectly.

### 7. Persist operator-facing trace references

Persist a compact telemetry projection for workflow, run, step execution, attempt, incident, control request, and Omnigent bridge session:

```text
traceId
rootSpanId or entrySpanId
correlationId
temporalWorkflowId / runId
backendName
traceUrl (server-generated from an allowlisted template)
logsUrl (server-generated from an allowlisted template)
telemetryAvailable / unavailableReason
```

Generate links server-side from validated templates; never accept arbitrary URLs from runtimes. Show **View trace**, **View correlated logs**, **Open Temporal**, and relevant artifact links in Workflow Detail/Chat. Hide or disable links with an explicit reason when telemetry is not configured or no exported span exists.

### 8. Sampling, privacy, and resilience

Define a production sampling strategy that retains errors, slow paths, retries, controls, recovery operations, and a bounded sample of successful runs. Ensure head/tail sampling decisions do not break propagation.

Add exporter queue/drop metrics, collector reachability health, redaction counters, and a test mode that exports to an in-memory collector. Collector/backend outages must not fail API requests, activities, or workflows.

### 9. Verification and rollout

Roll out in stages:

1. shared bootstrap and collector with export disabled by default;
2. API + Temporal propagation in non-production;
3. activity/provider/artifact/bridge spans;
4. Workflow Detail deep links;
5. production sampling and SLO/alert integration;
6. removal of legacy ad hoc trace-link generation.

Record before/after overhead and dropped-span behavior for representative workflows.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] One API request that starts a workflow produces an exported, navigable trace linked through Temporal client, workflow/run boundaries, activities, provider/managed-runtime or Omnigent bridge work, and artifact operations.
- [ ] Every API and worker process uses the shared telemetry bootstrap and has stable resource attributes.
- [ ] All Temporal client construction paths use propagation interceptors.
- [ ] Continue-As-New and child workflows preserve correlation without falsely presenting multiple Temporal runs as one physical run.
- [ ] Workflow Detail and incident/control surfaces show authorized trace/log deep links when configured and an explicit unavailable state otherwise.
- [ ] Managed direct-compatibility and Omnigent bridge spans use the same MoonMind execution correlation fields.
- [ ] No secret, raw prompt/completion, large log, diff, presigned URL, or unbounded user text is emitted to spans/logs by default.
- [ ] Collector/backend failure does not change workflow/API correctness and is visible through bounded health metrics.
- [ ] Span names/attributes, metric labels, and redaction behavior have automated contract tests.
- [ ] A hermetic integration test queries a test collector and proves trace continuity and required attributes across the full path.
- [ ] Performance tests document acceptable latency/CPU/memory overhead and exporter queue behavior.

## Test plan

Add unit tests for settings/bootstrap/idempotency, attribute sanitization, link-template validation, and propagation helpers; component tests for FastAPI and Temporal client wiring; Temporal replay tests; fake provider/Omnigent bridge instrumentation tests; collector-outage failure injection; and a combined-stack trace journey that asserts exported spans rather than only local trace IDs.
